subject: [PATCH v2 09/21] fuse: handle partial io passthrough for read/write, splice, and mmap

reviews.author: Amir Goldstein
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Amir Goldstein expressed discomfort with the removal of fuse_file_passthrough(ff) and suggested keeping the logic contained in a helper function for better documentation.

Reviewer Amir Goldstein suggested using the fuse_file_passthrough_op() helper in the call sites, which would simplify the code and eliminate the need for 'jumping back to direct io' in passthrough.c.

Reviewer Amir Goldstein suggested a more elegant way to handle the existing ENODEV case by changing the helper in the call site, which already covers this scenario.

reviews.author: Amir Goldstein
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Amir Goldstein noted that the fuse_inode_backing() function may not be initialized when the backing inode is set on open, and suggested initializing it to a struct fuse_backing pointer named fb

Reviewer Amir Goldstein noted that the condition for returning -ENODEV in fuse_passthrough_mmap() is unclear, as it does not account for cases where only GETATTR passthrough is enabled, and suggested relaxing the condition to check for FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_RW_OPS instead.
reviews.raw_body:
This needs to be:
         struct fuse_backing *fb = fuse_inode_backing(fi);

so the helper will work correctly whether the backing inode was set on
lookup (long lived) or on open.

---

This could technically be relaxed to:
          else if (fuse_passthrough_op(inode, FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_RW_OPS))
                 return -ENODEV;

However, I should note about this sketch patch that the condition for
fuse_passthrough_mmap()
is that both read/write are passthrough and the condition for ENODEV
is that either read/write are passthrough but looking at this code it is
not at all clear to the reader.

Because if there is no read/write passthrough, (only GETATTR passthrough)
there is not really a reason to prevent fuse page cache mmap.

See similar comment I made regarding fuse_file_cached_io_open() on the
documentation patch.

Thanks,
Amir.

2026-05-16.patch_summary: This patch allows servers to pass through only reads or writes instead of both, avoiding cache coherency issues by separating the backing file's page cache from the fuse inode's page cache. It also rejects mmap operations when passthrough is partial or not set for read/writes since mmap requires both read and write access. The patch includes self-contained fallback logic for handling direct io when passthrough is not enabled.
